title: SecureDatagramChannel pending_requests race on concurrent response handling
description:
File: python/src/lichen/coap/secure.py:346-347

In _unprotect(), the check-then-pop on pending_requests dict is not atomic:

    if is_response and msg.token in peer_ctx.pending_requests:
        request_id = peer_ctx.pending_requests.pop(msg.token)

Multiple _process_incoming() tasks can run concurrently (line 289 creates a new task per datagram). If two responses with the same token arrive simultaneously:

1. Task A: checks msg.token in pending_requests -> True
2. Task B: checks msg.token in pending_requests -> True
3. Task A: pop(msg.token) -> succeeds
4. Task B: pop(msg.token) -> KeyError (key no longer exists)

The pending_requests dict is accessed without synchronization, and the context_store lock only protects getting the PeerContext reference, not subsequent access to its fields.

This could cause crashes when processing duplicate/retransmitted responses or during network congestion with multiple in-flight responses.

Fix: Use dict.pop(key, None) instead of the check-then-pop pattern, or add per-context synchronization for pending_requests access.
